meaning: v.使能避免的,使心情紊乱n.失去平衡,紊乱
examples: Tax cuts may unbalance the budget.
His anxieties threatened to unbalance his mind.
Unbalance of shaft system is diagnosed by testing.
Looser controls would encourage more productive investments that are less likely to unbalance the world economy.
They have the unbalance that they have no-one to speak it at home and that's what I think we need to address.
